content: HFpEF diagnosis requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging data. HFA-PEFF algorithm: Step 1—pretest assessment (symptoms, signs, NT-proBNP >125 pg/mL or BNP >35 pg/mL, ECG); Step 2—echocardiography (structural: LV mass index ≥95 g/m² men or ≥85 g/m² women, LA volume index ≥34 mL/m²; functional: average E/e' ≥13, TR velocity >2.8 m/s, global longitudinal strain <16%); score ≥5 diagnostic. H2FPEF score: Heavy (BMI >30, 2 pts), Hypertensive (2+ antihypertensives, 1 pt), Atrial fibrillation (1 pt), Pulmonary hypertension (TR velocity >2.8 m/s, 1 pt), Elder (>60 years, 1 pt), Filling pressure (E/e' >9, 1 pt); score ≥6 high probability. Exercise testing with stress echo or invasive hemodynamics (gold standard—PCWP >15 mmHg at rest or >25 mmHg with exercise) for uncertain cases. Cardiac MRI for tissue characterization (fibrosis, amyloid).
